value.found_quote: To prevent `bypassPermissions` or `auto` mode from being used, set `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` or `permissions.disableAutoMode` to `"disable"` in any [settings file](/en/settings#settings-files). These are most useful in [managed settings](#managed-settings) where they cannot be overridden.
value.rationale: The page describes managed settings as a mechanism where administrators distribute permission policies that cannot be overridden, including disabling bypass mode (`disableBypassPermissionsMode`) and auto mode (`disableAutoMode`). It also references that administrators can prevent bypass mode via managed settings. This substantiates the claim about administrator-controlled, enterprise-wide locked-down permission policies that disable bypass/auto modes and restrict available permission modes.
